what_it_does: Acceptance test that verifies two things: the cognition_info projection exposes each role's C-build facets (op and input_addresses), and the engine's cast-beyond-listening behavior is data-driven—any role declaring a mode_scope that includes a non-listening mode (here 'walkthrough') is picked up by cast_for_mode and is fireable, with no hardcoded 'listening' gate in the firing path. It also creates a temporary role declaring mode_scope={'walkthrough'} to prove the engine fires it without touching committed roles.
observations: Uses a global PASS counter and ok flag with a check(label, cond) helper that prints ok/FAIL lines.
Bootstraps a Suite with a temp FsStore and discovers nodes from the 'nodes' directory.
Section 1 asserts cognition_info roles contain 'op' defaulting to 'generate' and 'input_addresses' as a list, using 'recall' as the example role.
Section 2 asserts mode_stages('walkthrough') is truthy, that every role in the walkthrough cast is fireable (vacuously true if empty), and that the listening cast is non-empty.

Creates a temp roles directory, writes a pydantic-backed ROLE dict with mode_scope={'walkthrough'}, discovers it with RoleRegistry, and checks cast_for_mode('walkthrough') includes and can fire the temp 'wt_probe' role.
Heavy inline commentary explains the test is proving an engine capability, not the content of a guided-review walkthrough cast, and references a prior 'transient-emptiness assertion' deadlock.
Calls sys.exit(0 if ok else 1) at the end.
standards: The test invents a temporary role rather than adding a committed one, keeping the acceptance check out of guided-review's content territory.
The commentary reframes the invariant from 'walkthrough cast must be empty' to 'whatever roles declare walkthrough must be fireable', which is the data-driven property being locked in.
It treats op/input_addresses as a one-source projection surface for roles, not front-end hardcoded.
conventions: Acceptance-test style with printed PASS/FAIL and a global counter.
Uses sys.path insertion and os.chdir(ROOT) to run from repo root.
Uses tempfile for isolated store and temp roles directory.
Imports runtime.roles.RoleRegistry separately to test role discovery in isolation.
concerns: Global mutable state (PASS, ok) and a check() helper that relies on side effects.
Temporary roles directory and temp store are created but not explicitly cleaned up.
Assumes 'recall' exists in roles and that 'nodes' directory is present and discoverable.
Uses getattr(r, 'can_fire', False) without defining what can_fire means in this file.
Calls sys.exit, which can terminate the test runner if imported or executed in an unexpected context.
notes: An acceptance test that locks in the engine's data-driven cast behavior and the role-shape projection, using a throwaway walkthrough role to avoid planting content in guided-review's space.
questions: What does 'C 4/4' refer to in the docstring and final print?
What is the exact contract of mode_stages and cast_for_mode on the Suite/RoleRegistry?
What does can_fire evaluate, and what makes a role fireable?
Which nodes are expected to be discovered from the 'nodes' directory?
Is the cognition_info projection guaranteed to include op defaulting to 'generate' for every role, or only for roles that do not override it?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the actual content: it is an acceptance test proving data-driven cast-beyond-listening and the exposure of op/input_addresses in cognition_info.
apparent_themes: Data-driven engine behavior over hardcoded modes
Separation of engine capability from content declaration
intention_signals: The file is trying to prevent regression of a newly enabled engine capability by proving that non-listening casts are driven purely by role declarations, and to give guided-review a green light to add walkthrough roles on top of this capability without further engine changes.
novelty: It uses a dynamically written, temporary role file to acceptance-test a mode cast without committing any role to the repository, which is unusual for an acceptance test.
contribution: Provides an acceptance check that the cast_for_mode mechanism is data-driven and that role build facets (op, input_addresses) are visible through cognition_info, which a fused system would keep as a regression guard for the engine's firing path and role projection.
summary_for_embedding: Acceptance test verifying that cast_for_mode is data-driven for non-listening modes and that cognition_info exposes role op and input_addresses, using a temporary walkthrough-scoped role as a probe.
intention_for_embedding: Lock in the engine capability to fire any role-declared mode cast and to expose full role shape to surfaces, without hardcoding listening-specific behavior.

symbols.does: Acceptance-style assertion helper that prints ok/FAIL, increments PASS on success, and sets the global ok flag to False on failure.
symbols.description: A simple global check function used throughout the test to record pass/fail state. It prints a formatted line for each label/condition pair, increments the shared PASS counter when the condition is true, and flips the shared ok flag to False when it is not, supporting the script's final exit-code decision.
